beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and grab breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: Felix wakes at home in Beacon Hill after yesterday's low energy (45/100). Morning routine folded into one beat; no bagel stop today given yesterday's bagel skepticism and the heat forecast—he'll eat light at home.

beats.activity: Commute to First Hill studio for freelance photography work.
beats.reasoning: Felix heads to his workspace in First Hill for a product shoot; light traffic on a Wednesday morning, quick commute via I-5.
Felix is booked for a morning shoot; staying indoors during the building heat keeps him productive and hydrated before the worst of the afternoon sun hits.

beats.activity: Lunch and rest at a local café, monitoring air quality.
beats.reasoning: Felix grabs lunch and checks the air quality index; the wildfire smoke is moderate, so he's cautious about exertion but not confined. A familiar spot to decompress between shoots.

beats.activity: Errand: camera equipment maintenance and film stock pickup.
beats.reasoning: Heat is peaking (91°F), so Felix runs his essential errand indoors; Glazer's is a trusted spot for gear maintenance and supplies. The errand keeps him occupied during the worst heat window.

beats.activity: Commute back to Beacon Hill; stop for hydration and rest.
beats.reasoning: Felix heads home after the errand, timing the commute to beat rush hour and escape the heat. He'll rest up before the evening concert.

beats.activity: Rest, hydrate, and prep for evening; check weather and concert details.
beats.reasoning: Felix cools down at home with water and a light snack, playing a video game to recharge mentally. He's introverted and values smaller gatherings, but a free outdoor concert on a clear evening appeals to him; he mentally prepares for crowds.

beats.activity: Commute to Gas Works Park for outdoor concert.
beats.reasoning: Clear skies and the concert starts at 6pm; Felix heads north early to claim a quieter spot away from the main crowd and catch the sunset before the music starts.

beats.activity: Enjoy free outdoor concert and people-watch.
beats.reasoning: Felix attends the concert from 6pm; he stays for about 1.5 hours, soaking in the clear evening and live music. He brings a camera to capture the light and scene, then heads out before the crowd becomes overwhelming.

beats.activity: Commute to The Crooked Table for evening bartending shift.
beats.reasoning: Felix leaves the concert early to avoid crowds and heads to his bartending job; the timing allows him to arrive for a 9pm shift setup with buffer time.

beats.activity: Bartending shift at The Crooked Table.
beats.reasoning: Felix works a busy Wednesday evening shift; the bar is likely bustling post-concert crowd. He's in his element here, making drinks and connecting with regulars.

beats.activity: Commute home and wind down with tea and stargazing.
beats.reasoning: Felix heads home after his shift; the clear skies mean he finally gets his stargazing moment. He makes tea and sits outside on the porch, enjoying the cool night before bed.
Felix sleeps after a full day of work, errands, and evening activity. The beat spans overnight, ending at 6am as required.

diary: Woke up to another scorcher—91 degrees by afternoon, but the sky was so clear I couldn't resist shooting at Gas Works later. The morning studio work felt solid; got through a product shoot without too many retakes, then grabbed lunch at Café Solstice and checked the air quality obsessively. The wildfire smoke from BC is creeping in, but nothing terrible yet. Picked up fresh film stock at Glazer's, which always feels good. The concert at Gas Works was the real highlight though—caught maybe forty minutes of the free show with the Space Needle glowing in the distance, watching people sprawl on blankets. The crowd was genuinely happy, and I got some candid shots I'm excited to develop. The bartending shift at The Crooked Table flew by; Wednesday crowd was mellow. Came home and just sat with tea, watching the haze settle over the city. Felt like one of those days where everything clicked.
thought_process: I knew it was going to be hot and I wanted to maximize the light for both work and play, so I front-loaded the studio shoot and left breathing room for the concert. The air quality kept nagging at me—I'm not usually paranoid about it, but that BC smoke has been creeping in all week, so I checked a few times. I almost skipped Gas Works because of the heat and smoke, but honestly, I needed to be around people and capture something spontaneous. The bartending gig felt like a nice wind-down after being out in the sun. By evening I was tired but satisfied, not the restless kind of tired. The day had a natural rhythm to it—work, play, work again—and I leaned into it.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
